data.title: 99 Speed Mart, Dialog, Keyfield, Swift Haulage, Samchem, PBA, Fibromat, Bintai Kinden, TNB, BTM Resources
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): Here is a brief recap of some corporate announcements and happenings that made the headlines on Thursday:

Home-grown mini-market operator 99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Bhd (KL:99SMART) posted a 7.53% year-on-year (y-o-y) increase in net profit to RM143.18 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025, from RM133.15 million, driven by stronger retail sales and network expansion. Quarterly revenue rose 7.66% to RM2.61 billion from RM2.43 billion. It declared its first interim dividend of 2.25 sen per share, payable on June 9. With a dividend policy of distributing approximately 50% of its net profit, 99 Speed Mart intends to declare dividends twice annually. It registered a net addition of 246 outlets y-o-y, bringing its total store count to 2,833 as of end-March. — 99 Speed Mart kicks off FY2025 with stronger sales in 1Q, pays 2.25 sen dividend

Integrated oil and gas services provider Dialog Group Bhd’s (KL:DIALOG) net profit for the January-March quarter dropped 13.57% y-o-y to RM134.97 million, from RM156.16 million, as revenue fell on lower performances from both its Malaysian and international operations. Quarterly revenue fell 17.57% to RM578.81 million — its weakest since 2QFY2022, when the topline was RM544.49 million — from RM702.2 million.  It declared an interim cash dividend of 1.3 sen per share, payable on June 26. Despite the weaker y-o-y showing, Dialog's 3QFY2025 was a turnaround for the group compared to 2QFY2025 when it incurred a net loss of RM129.49 million, even as revenue dropped 14.9% from RM680 million. — Dialog's 3Q profit drops 14% as revenue skids to over three-year low

Keyfield International Bhd’s (KL:KEYFIELD) net profit fell 31.7% for 1QFY2025 to RM20.68 million from RM30.3 million, dragged by lower fleet utilisation rates, increased off-hire costs and maintenance fees. The offshore support vessel provider said the first quarter of each year is traditionally its weakest quarter due to seasonal monsoon, and so the majority of its offshore activities, especially maintenance, are carried out.  Quarterly revenue fell 18.5% to RM86.75 million from RM106.39 million, with fleet utilisation dropping to 44.1% in 1QFY2025 from 67.1% in the same period last year. Quarterly revenue fell 18.5% to RM86.75 million from RM106.39 million, with fleet utilisation dropping to 44.1% in 1QFY2025 from 67.1% last year. It declared a dividend of one sen per share for 1QFY2025, to be paid on June 13. Its order book now stands at RM446.7 million, of which RM283.5 million is to be realised in FY2025. — Keyfield International’s 1Q profit falls 32% amid lower fleet utilisation

Swift Haulage Bhd (KL:SWIFT) posted a 65.9% drop in net profit to RM7.22 million from RM21.14 million last year, due to the absence of a one-off disposal gain that it booked a year ago. Revenue edged up 3.5% to RM185.68 million in 1QFY2025 versus RM179.35 million previously. No dividend was declared for the quarter. — Swift Haulage's 1Q profit falls 65.9% on absence of disposal gain

Having posted a 34.2% drop in first-quarter net profit, chemical supplier Samchem Holdings Bhd (KL:SAMCHEM) expects a more challenging operating environment in 2025 due to global trade disruptions, volatile foreign exchange rates and rising oversupply in the industry. It said increasing production capacity in China has worsened oversupply conditions, placing further pressure on prices and margins across the region. For 1QFY2025, it reported a net profit of RM4.01 million, down from RM6.10 million a year ago. Revenue was also down 3.5% y-o-y to RM287.76 million from RM298.24 million, mainly due to a decrease in average selling prices. — Samchem cautious on outlook after lower 1Q profit on soft margins

PBA Holdings Bhd (KL:PBA), Penang’s water supply operator, posted a 36.1% increase in net profit to RM19.54 million for 1QFY2025 from RM14.35 million a year ago, underpinned by the water tariff increase that took effect in February last year. Revenue increased 11.8% to RM124.28 million from RM111.17 million previously. Looking forward, it said revenue is expected to increase in line with Penang’s rapid industrialisation, which will result in an increase in the sale of treated water. — Penang water supply operator PBA's net profit rises 36% y-o-y after tariff adjustment

Geotechnical solutions specialist Fibromat (M) Bhd (KL:FIBRO) has bagged a RM283.5 million contract from Casa Hartamas Sdn Bhd to provide design and installation solutions. The contract, which was secured by its unit MTS Fibromat (M) Sdn Bhd, involves the supply of materials, labour, machinery, and other essential resources for the execution and completion of works related to the Central Spine Road Package 2 and Section 2A. The project will cover road construction from Sungai Lakit Bridge to Gua Musang and from Sungai Lakit Bridge to Paloh 2. — Fibromat bags RM283.5 mil road job in Kelantan

Bintai Kinden Corp Bhd (KL:BINTAI) said Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) (KL:TENAGA) has reinstated three of 10 contracts that the utility company terminated two years ago, after a series of negotiations between them. The reinstated contracts, executed by Bintai Kinden's unit Kejuruteraan Bintai Kindenko Sdn Bhd (KBK), are related to defect clearance works at PMU 275kV/33kV Grik, the 132kV Subang Jaya GIS Switching Station, and the 132kV Kuchai Lama GIS Switching Station. TNB's terminations in 2023 were reported to be linked to Bintai Kinden’s financial difficulties, which arose after the suspension or withdrawal of its banking facilities. — Bintai Kinden says TNB has reinstated three contracts it terminated two years ago

BTM Resources Bhd (KL:BTM) has called off its plan to build a 10MW biomass power plant after the banking facilities it obtained for the project was cancelled. It is also considering whether to cancel another 7M biomass power plant, which it has put on hold for now. Besides the cancellation of banking facilities in February this year, it cited current market volatilities, including raw material price fluctuations as reasons behind its decision. The sawmill operator said Sustainable Energy Development Authority Malaysia had on May 8 agreed to its application to relinquish the feed-in-tariff approval certificate for the 10MW biomass power plant. — BTM Resources cancels 10MW biomass plant after losing bank funding, shelves another plant

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: SingTel to sell US$1b stake in Bharti Airtel via block deals — report
data.description:
(May 15): Singapore Telecommunications is expected to sell shares worth US$1 billion (RM4.3 billion) in Indian telecom major Bharti Airtel on Friday through block deals, the Business Standard newspaper reported on Thursday, citing sources.

Southeast Asia's largest telecom operator, SingTel, is looking to sell 47.6 million Airtel shares at a floor price of 1,800 rupees a share, a 3.6% discount to the stock's last close, the report said.

JPMorgan is likely to be the broker for the deal, it added.

SingTel has been reducing its stake in Airtel for a while. In March last year, it sold shares worth US$711 million to GQG Partners.

It held a 9.49% stake in Airtel through its affiliate Pastel Ltd, according to exchange data as of March-quarter.

Airtel and SingTel did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comments.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Wall St edges lower as trade truce rally fizzles, UnitedHealth falls
data.description:
(May 15): Wall Street dipped on Thursday as elation from the US-China tariff truce faded for major indices, while UnitedHealth's stock took a pummeling after a report of a US Department of Justice (DOJ) fraud inquiry into the insurer.

UnitedHealth Group plunged 16% to its lowest level since April 2020.

The Wall Street Journal reported that the DOJ was conducting a criminal investigation into the company for possible Medicare fraud. However, the health insurer said it had not been informed of a criminal probe by federal prosecutors.

Walmart will have to start raising prices later this month due to the high cost of tariffs, executives said, even as the retail giant's US comparable sales surpassed expectations in the first quarter. Its shares were down 4.8%

At 9.42am, the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 128.62 points, or 0.31%, to 41,922.44, the S&P 500 lost 19.46 points, or 0.33%, to 5,873.03, and the Nasdaq Composite lost 123.27 points, or 0.64%, to 19,023.53.

Speaking on the day, US Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell said central bank officials felt they needed to reconsider the key elements around jobs as well as inflation in their current monetary policy approach.

US retail sales growth slowed in April, while a Labor Department report showed the producer price index for final demand fell 0.5% for the same month, compared to an expectation of a 0.2% rise.

On an annual basis, producer prices came in at 2.4% versus an estimate of 2.5%.

"There will be a hump and pick up in prices, but until we see how big that is and how lasting that is, the Fed should be able to remain patient," said Jan Nevruzi, US rates strategist at TD Securities.

The data dump follows a relatively tame consumer price reading earlier this week, indicating that consumer prices rebounded moderately last month.

In results-driven moves, Cisco Systems gained 2.9% after the networking-equipment maker raised its annual forecasts and named Mark Patterson its new CFO.

Only four out of the 11 S&P 500 sectors were trading in the green.

The energy sector fell the most, as oil prices slid around 3% on expectations of a US-Iran nuclear deal that could result in sanctions easing.

Stocks have been see-sawing this week as equities jumped on Monday and Tuesday following a temporary ceasefire in the US-China tariff war. The gains were enough to drag the S&P out of the red for the year — its first positive showing since late February — although it is still about 4% shy of its record highs.

Many megacap and growth stocks pulled back, with Nvidia slipping 1.2%, while Tesla shed 2.8%.

Foot Locker, soared 83.6% after rival Dick's Sporting Goods agreed to buy the footwear retailer for US$2.4 billion (RM10.31 billion).

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 1.01-to-1 ratio on the NYSE, while declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.28-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted four new 52-week highs and three new lows, while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 17 new highs and 51 new lows.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: PeterLabs shareholders call for EGM to remove seven directors amid internal probe
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): PeterLabs Holdings Bhd (KL:PLABS) has received a notice request from two substantial shareholders to convene an extraordinary general meeting (EGM) to remove seven directors and replace them with two new nominees.

In a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Thursday, the animal health and nutrition products group said the requisition came from Bu Yaw Seng and Datin Lin Ching Yein, who collectively hold more than a 10% stake in the company. 

The requisitionists are seeking to remove the six existing directors — Lim Tong Seng, Teo Chin Heng, Yap Siaw Peng, Datuk Lim Tai Soon, Datuk Ng Boon Siong and Ho Siew Li — as well as Ng Kau, who is an alternate director to Boon Siong.

They also proposed the removal of any individual who may be appointed to the board to fill casual vacancies from the date of the requisition until the EGM is held.

The shareholders are calling for the EGM to be convened “with special notice and the soonest possible”, to vote on the proposed boardroom overhaul.

In their place, the requisitionists are nominating Datuk Wira Pua Kim An and Datuk Seri Garry Chua Kah Seng as new directors of the company.

“Subject to appropriate legal advice, the board will take the necessary required action accordingly and will make the appropriate announcement in due course,” PeterLabs said in the filing.

The shareholder action comes just two days after the company suspended Datuk Loh Saw Foong, its executive director and general manager of subsidiary Thye On Tong Trading Sdn Bhd, pending the outcome of an internal investigation into unspecified alleged misconduct. His suspension took effect from May 13 for a period of two weeks.

Saw Foong and independent non-executive director Loh Poh Im are the only two directors who are not on the “removal list”.

PeterLabs is involved in the manufacturing, distribution, export, and trading of animal health and nutrition products.

Shares in PeterLabs closed one sen or 3.13% higher at 33 sen on Thursday, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM90.82 million. Year-to-date, the stock has gained 73%.

Read also:
Peterlabs suspends executive director for two weeks for internal probe

Edited ByLee Weng Khuen

data.title: Court conducts Sapura Holdings' winding-up hearing in private after Shahril's lawyer sought protective order
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): The judge hearing the winding-up petition of Sapura Holdings Sdn Bhd on Thursday ordered the public gallery to be cleared of bystanders, including members of the media. Only a few select individuals were allowed to remain.

This occurred as the feuding brothers, Tan Sri Shahril Shamsuddin and Datuk Shahriman Shamsuddin, agreed to a protective order to restrict others from being in court while their father, Tan Sri Shamsuddin Abdul Kadir, continued his testimony. Shamsuddin is also the founder of Sapura Holdings.

At the start of proceedings on Thursday before Judge Leong Wai Hong, Shahril’s lawyer, Rabindra S Nathan said his client had filed a protective order with the court on April 3 to limit who could access court documents during the trial.

This was not opposed by Shahriman's lawyer, Gopal Sreeneevasan, Rabindra said. He then read out a list of people who were allowed to be in court, besides Shahril, Shahriman and their business partner, Datuk Rameli Musa.

“Because both parties are in agreement with the protective order, I will have to clear the court,” the judge then said.

Rabindra only began his cross-examination of Shamsuddin once the courtroom was cleared.

The next hearing of the case is scheduled for May 21, according to a source close to the matter. The public gallery will reopen after a brief continuation of Shamsuddin's cross-examination, the source said.

The court proceedings stemmed from a petition Shahriman filed on Sept 23 last year to wind up Sapura Holdings, claiming it was necessary for a fair distribution of assets. Shahril opposed this, arguing that it is not a family company.

Shahriman and Shahril own a 40.5% direct stake each in Sapura Holdings, while their equally owned entity, Brothers Capital Sdn Bhd, holds 15%. Rameli owns the remaining 4% interest in the company.

Idza Hajar Ahmad Idzam from Zul Rafique & Partners represented Sapura Holdings, while Rameli was represented by Ranjit Singh of Ranjit Singh & Yeoh.
 

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: China's Xiaomi to launch self-developed mobile chip in late May
data.description:
BEIJNG (May 15): Chinese smartphone and electric vehicle maker Xiaomi said on Thursday it would launch a new self-developed mobile phone chip, XringO1, in late May.

The announcement was made by Xiaomi's CEO Lei Jun on his Weibo account. He did not give any further details.

The move marks an effort by Xiaomi to develop an advanced mobile processor, as it has been focused on simpler chips such as power management chips and image sensors used on smartphones.

Competition in China’s smartphone market has been intensifying, with rivals like Huawei and Apple leveraging their own custom-designed chips to create tightly integrated ecosystems and enhance user experience.

Apple started designing its own custom chips in 2010, with the introduction of the A4 chip in the first-generation iPad and iPhone 4.

As of this year most of its products use Apple silicon including the M series for Macs and iPads, the A series for iPhones and iPads, and the S series for Apple Watch.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: BTM Resources cancels 10MW biomass plant after losing bank funding, shelves another plant
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): BTM Resources Bhd (KL:BTM) has called off its plan to build a 10MW biomass power plant after the banking facilities it obtained for the project was cancelled. It is also considering whether to cancel another 7M biomass power plant, which it has put on hold for now.

The sawmill operator said Sustainable Energy Development Authority Malaysia had on May 8 agreed to its application to relinquish the feed-in-tariff (FIT) approval certificate for the 10MW biomass power plant, it said in a bourse filing Thursday.

Besides the cancellation of banking facilities in February this year, it cited current market volatilities, including raw material price fluctuations as reasons behind its decision.

The relinquishment of the FIT approval took effect on April 30. The original approval, granted in July 2020, allowed the group to build, own and operate the renewable energy plant as well as supply electricity to Tenaga Nasional Bhd (KL:TENAGA) at a fixed tariff rate of RM0.3486 per kilowatt-hour for 21 years.

BTM said the withdrawal of its FIT would not materially impact its operations, as the biomass power plant had not commenced construction.

The group still holds another FIT approval, granted in May 2022, for the 7MW biomass power plant. It is reassessing the plant's potential utilisation and may consider relinquishing the FIT approval for it if prevailing market conditions persist in the next six months.

“Therefore, the biomass power plant project is presently put on hold,” BTM Resources said, adding it is looking at alternate uses and potential business ventures for the project land, and that details have yet to be finalised.

BTM Resources' share price settled unchanged at four sen on Thursday, valuing the group at RM50 million.

Read also:
BTM Resources receives approval to operate 10MW power plant
BTM Resources gets more time for biomass power plant

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: Norges Bank ceases to be substantial shareholder of Pentamaster after less than a month
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): In less than a month after emerging as a substantial shareholder of Pentamaster Corp Bhd (KL:PENTA), Norges Bank has now ceased to be a substantial shareholder in the automated test equipment maker.

Norges Bank, the manager of Norway’s government pension fund, sold 2.4 million shares, or a 0.34% stake, in Pentamaster on Wednesday, a bourse filing on Thursday showed. Another 417,200 shares, or a 0.06% stake, were “lent out” by Norges Bank.

The filing did not disclose the price at which the shares were traded. Based on Pentamaster’s May 14 closing price of RM2.89, the 0.4% stake was valued at RM8.15 million.  

Norges Bank’s stake in Pentamaster was trimmed to 4.87%, below the 5% threshold to be deemed a substantial shareholder.

Norges Bank emerged as a substantial shareholder of Pentamaster back on April 17 and saw its stake peak at 5.27% on May 5.

Pentamaster’s substantial shareholders comprise the Employees Provident Fund with a 9.83% stake, Kumpulan Wang Persaraan (Diperbadankan) (8.17%), and Lembaga Tabung Haji (5.48%).

Shares in Pentamaster ended five sen or 1.73% lower at RM2.84 on Thursday, valuing the group at RM2.02 billion.

Read also:
Norges Bank emerges as substantial shareholder of Pentamaster

Edited ByLee Weng Khuen

data.title: US set to cut capital requirements for banks — report
data.description:
(May 15): US authorities are preparing to announce one of the biggest cuts in banks' capital requirements in over a decade, the Financial Times reported on Thursday.

Regulators were poised to reduce the supplementary leverage ratio (SLR) in the next few months, the newspaper reported, citing several people familiar with the matter.

The supplementary leverage ratio is a rule that requires big US banks to keep an extra layer of loss-absorbing capital.

The US banking industry is optimistic that regulators will soon move to change how much capital they set aside against typically safe investments, particularly after the turmoil in Treasury markets last month.

A move to revamp the SLR could reduce the amount of cash banks must reserve, freeing them up for more lending or other activities, and could incentivize banks to play a larger role in intermediating Treasury markets.

US regulators have flagged the SLR as meriting reconsideration and are mulling whether to tweak the rule's formula to reduce big banks' burdens or provide relief for extremely safe investments, such as treasuries.

The Federal Reserve and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation declined to comment on the report, while theOffice of the Comptroller of the Currency did not respond to Reuters' request for comment.

Reuters could not immediately confirm the report.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Penang water supply operator PBA's net profit rises 36% y-o-y after tariff adjustment
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): PBA Holdings Bhd (KL:PBA), Penang’s water supply operator, posted a 36.1% year-on-year jump in net profit for the first quarter, underpinned by the water tariff increase that took effect in February last year.

Net profit for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) rose to RM19.54 million from RM14.35 million in the same period last year, according to the group's bourse filing on Thursday.

Revenue increased 11.8% to RM124.28 million versus RM111.17 million previously, which PBA attributed primarily to the increase in water tariffs for domestic users from Feb 1, 2024. 
 
No dividend was declared for the quarter.

Last February, domestic users in Penang saw their water tariffs double — with those using 20-35 cubic metres a month being charged RM1.10, up from 46 sen previously, and those using more than 35 cubic metres having to pay RM2 per cubic metre, compared with 68 sen previously. But accounts that use zero to 20 cubic metres a month saw their rates raised to 62 sen per cubic metre from 22 sen. 

This came after the National Water Services Commission, or SPAN, announced that water tariffs for domestic users in the peninsula and Labuan will be raised by an average 22 sen per cubic metre.

Hence, for 1QFY2025, PBA recorded an additional month of higher water tariffs, compared with the two months that were recognised in 1QFY2024.

Looking forward, PBA said revenue is expected to increase in line with Penang’s rapid industrialisation, which will result in an increase in the sale of treated water.

Shares in PBA ended three sen or 1.4% lower at RM2.12, valuing the group at RM702.29 million.

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: Duopharma posts record-high 1Q profit on strong sales, lower ingredient costs
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): Duopharma Biotech Bhd (KL:DPHARMA) posted a 67.8% year-on-year jump in net profit to a record high in the first quarter of the year, thanks to strong sales coupled with industry-wide normalisation of active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) prices.

Net profit for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) climbed to RM25.64 million from RM15.28 million in the same period last year, according to the pharmaceutical outfit’s bourse filing on Thursday.

This was on the back of revenue having increased 36.2% to RM262.74 million versus RM192.97 million in 1QFY2024 amid strong performance across all business sectors, with a notable surge in insulin sales to fulfil outstanding orders to regularise supply.

“At the same time, industry-wide normalisation of API prices to a pre-pandemic level also contributed to profit growth,” the group added.

No dividend was declared for the quarter.

Backed by the strong start to the financial year, Duopharma group managing director Leonard Ariff Abdul Shatar said the group sees a favourable domestic market but remains vigilant in monitoring the potential impact of global economic uncertainties, including the US tariff policy.

Leonard said Duopharma will remain focused on enhancing operational efficiencies, optimising cost management strategies, and adapting to evolving market conditions to remain competitive in the face of rising costs.

The group is contracted to supply a total of 100 products with a combined estimated value of about RM684.15 million until Dec 31, 2026.

Shares in Duopharma ended three sen or 2.38% higher at RM1.29 on Thursday, valuing the group at RM1.24 billion.

Edited ByLee Weng Khuen

data.title: 99 Speed Mart kicks off FY2025 with stronger sales in 1Q, pays 2.25 sen dividend
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): Home-grown mini-market operator 99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Bhd (KL:99SMART) posted a 7.53% year-on-year (y-o-y) increase in net profit to RM143.18 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), driven by stronger retail sales and network expansion.

Earnings per share came in at 1.7 sen, up from 1.59 sen a year earlier, according to the group’s filing with Bursa Malaysia. Revenue saw the same growth pace, up 7.66% to RM2.61 billion from RM2.43 billion in 1QFY2024.

The group declared its first interim dividend of 2.25 sen per share, amounting to a total payout of RM189 million, which will be paid on June 9. With a dividend policy of distributing approximately 50% of net profit, it intends to declare dividends twice annually.

As at end-1Q FY2025, 99 Speed Mart had cash and bank balances of RM779.62 million.

“We are pleased to kick off the financial year with a solid set of results, reflecting our ongoing expansion and the growing traction of 99 Bulksales,” said founder and chief executive officer Lee Thiam Wah in a statement.

To support future growth, the group said it expects to commence operations at its new distribution centre (DC) in Cyberjaya, Selangor by the second quarter of 2025, following the commissioning of its Miri, Sarawak DC in March.

The Cyberjaya facility, with a built-up area of approximately 120,000 square feet, will serve around 200 outlets in the region and further bolster the group's bulk sales e-commerce capabilities.

“As we continue to scale our outlet network and enhance accessibility, our commitment remains on offering affordable and convenient daily essentials to communities nationwide, which is in line with our NEAR n’ SAVE ethos,” Lee added.

During the quarter, total sales transactions rose 7.8% y-o-y to 119.6 million, with the average basket size stable at RM21.80. The group also registered a net addition of 246 outlets y-o-y, bringing its total store count to 2,833 as of March 31, 2025.

Shares in 99 Speed Mart closed four sen or 1.79% higher at RM2.19 on Thursday, giving the group a market capitalisation of RM18.4 billion. The counter is down nearly 11% year-to-date.

Edited ByLee Weng Khuen

data.title: Coinbase expects up to US$400m hit from cyber attack
data.description:
(May 15): Coinbase forecast a hit between US$180 million (RM771.8 million) and US$400 million from a cyber attack that breached account data of a "small subset" of its customers, sending the crypto exchange's shares down 3% in pre-market trading on Thursday.

The company said it received an email from an unknown threat actor on May 11, claiming to have information about certain customer accounts as well as internal documents.

The disclosure comes days before the company is set to join the benchmark S&P 500 index, marking a landmark moment for the crypto industry.

Coinbase said that while the attackers stole some data including names, addresses and emails, they did not get access to login credentials or passwords. It will, however, reimburse the customers who were tricked into sending funds to the attackers.

The hackers had paid multiple contractors and employees working in support roles outside the US to collect information from internal systems. Coinbase has fired the employees involved immediately, it said.

It also refused to pay the ransom demand of US$20 million and is working with law enforcement agencies. It has instead established a US$20 million reward for information on the attackers.

"Instead of funding criminal activity, we have investigated the incident, reinforced our controls, and will reimburse customers impacted by this incident," the company said in a blog post.

Security remains a challenge for the crypto industry. In February, Bybit disclosed that attackers had stolen digital tokens worth around US$1.5 billion, which many called the biggest crypto heist of all time.

Funds stolen by hacking crypto platforms totaled US$2.2 billion in 2024, according to a report from blockchain analysis firm Chainalysis, the fourth straight year where such hacks have topped more than US$1 billion.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Dialog's 3Q profit drops 14% as revenue skids to over three-year low
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): Integrated oil and gas services provider Dialog Group Bhd’s (KL:DIALOG) net profit for the January-March quarter dropped 13.57% year-on-year, as revenue fell on lower performances from both its Malaysian and international operations.

Net profit for the third quarter ended March 31, 2024 (3QFY2025) fell to RM134.97 million from RM156.16 million in 3QFY2024, its bourse filing on Thursday showed. Earnings per share dropped to 2.39 sen from 2.77 sen.

Lower oil prices dragged down its Malaysian operations’ revenue and profit contribution for 3QFY2025, while its international operations saw a reduction in business activities, particularly after the group divested its entire 60% stake in the Dialog Jubail Supply Base in Saudi Arabia.

Quarterly revenue fell 17.57% to RM578.81 million — its weakest since 2QFY2022, when the topline was RM544.49 million — from RM702.2 million.  

It declared an interim cash dividend of 1.3 sen per share, payable on June 26, bringing FY2025's year-to-date payout to 1.3 sen per share, down 13.33% from 1.5 sen in the same period in FY2023.

Despite the weaker year-on-year showing, Dialog's 3QFY2025 was a turnaround for the group compared to the previous quarter of 2QFY2025, despite a 14.9% drop in revenue from RM680 million.

Dialog had incurred a net loss of RM129.49 million in 2QFY2025 — its first quarterly loss in 25 years — mainly due to one-off impairment of investments amounting to RM134.72 million and project cost overruns.

For the first nine months of FY2025, Dialog made a net profit of RM156.44 million, down 64.17% from RM436.62 million in the same period of FY2024, as revenue fell 19.16% to RM1.89 billion from RM2.34 billion.

On prospects, Dialog said increased upstream activities are providing it the opportunity to participate in the field development cycle value chain, particularly in the provision of engineering and specialist technical services.

For its midstream business, Dialog said the segment remains a core focus, where it will undertake phased capacity expansions in Pengerang Deepwater Terminals (PDT). Within PDT, a Dialog and Petronas Gas Bhd (KL:PETGAS) joint venture has commenced work on the liquefied natural gas-driven air separation unit facility.

In the downstream business, the group said it will be cautious and take a selective approach to bidding for engineering, procurement, commissioning and construction contracts, and will prioritise in-house projects.

It also noted that its associate Morimatsu Dialog (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd has completed its RM250 million fabrication facilities in Pengerang, Johor, that provide one-stop technical and fabrication solutions for specialised process modules and skids for the energy, chemical, pharmaceutical, solar power and data centre industries.

“As the economic environment is expected to remain challenging in the short to medium term, we will continue to build and strengthen our competencies by investing in and upskilling our workforce, and digital transformation to ensure we remain efficient and competitive,” it added.

Dialog’s share price closed up five sen or 2.91% to RM1.77, bringing the stock a market capitalisation of RM9.99 billion. Over the past one-year, the stock has declined 28%.

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: High Court orders Serendib Capital to pay RM200,000 as security for costs or lose case against Sarawak Cable, Mahmud Abu Bekir
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): The High Court has ruled that UK-based Serendib Capital Ltd must pay RM200,000 as security for costs in its lawsuit against Datuk Seri Mahmud Abu Bekir Taib, Sarawak Cable Bhd (KL:SCABLE), and six others.

The payment must be made within 14 days from May 13, or Serendib’s case will be struck out.

According to a court order seen by The Edge, Judicial Commissioner Dr Suzana Muhamad Said approved Mahmud Abu Bekir’s request for security costs and ordered Serendib to pay RM200,000 within 14 days from May 13. If Serendib fails to do so, its lawsuit against Mahmud Abu Bekir will be struck out.

On March 20, Mahmud Abu Bekir filed an application through his lawyers, Messrs Dinesh Ratnarajah Partnership, asking the court to order Serendib to provide security costs. He argued that Serendib is a UK-based company with only £100 (RM569) in paid-up capital and no assets in Malaysia.

“Furthermore, Serendib is the only plaintiff in the suit, and should it be ordered to pay costs, there are no others that could be relied on,” Mahmud Abu Bekir, who is also a director of Sarawak Cable, said.

Mahmud Abu Bekir was represented by Ashok Kandiah and Dinesh, Tan Jun Yu appeared for Serendib while Janice Tay Shu Hui appeared for Sarawak Cable.

In May last year, Serendib sued Sarawak Cable, Mahmud Abu Bekir and six other directors, claiming they conspired to harm the company. Serendib is seeking RM6.2 million in damages and RM300,000 for managing Sarawak Cable and its subsidiaries. It is also asking the court to award punitive and aggravated damages.

The UK-based company, which focuses on environmental, social and governance investments and blue carbon initiatives, claimed it was brought in to help restructure Sarawak Cable’s debts after the company fell into Practice Note 17 status in September 2022.

Serendib said it signed a memorandum of agreement (MOA) in December 2023 to act as Sarawak Cable’s “white knight”. The company argued the MOA was binding on Mahmud Abu Bekir, who it claims is the controlling shareholder.

It said it fulfilled its duties under the MOA, but Mahmud Abu Bekir disagreed, saying the company failed to meet key milestones. A notice to terminate the MOA was issued on April 30, 2024.

“The termination of the MOA is unlawful and amounts to a wrongful repudiation of the MOA,” Serendib alleged.

Mahmud Abu Bekir, in his defence, argued that Serendib’s efforts were of no value because Sarawak Cable didn’t reach any deals with its creditors as promised. He said Serendib failed to meet legal requirements under the Contracts Act. An audit by CEO Russel Walter Boyd found no action was taken by Serendib, and Sarawak Cable’s board agreed to end the agreement with the company.

Read also:
Sarawak Cable to sue unit’s liquidators over alleged misconduct in asset disposal

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: LBS Bina partnering Oriental Holdings for RM7b mixed development in Melaka
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): Property developer LBS Bina Group Bhd (KL:LBS) is collaborating with Penang-based Oriental Holdings Bhd (KL:ORIENT) to undertake a mixed-use project in Klebang, Melaka, with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM7 billion.

A memorandum of understanding (MOU) has been signed for the proposed joint-venture development. 

According to both companies’ filings with the stock exchange on Thursday, the proposed development will span approximately 561 acres and be executed in four phases over a 15-year period.

The project aims to transform the land into a mixed-use development featuring both industrial and commercial components.

Under the terms of the agreement, Oriental Holdings — being the landowner —` will be entitled to receive either 17% of the GDV generated from property unit sales, calculated at a minimum of RM50 per square foot or RM1.2 billion in total, whichever amount is higher.

The development site is located in a coastal area, which is about 9km from Tanjung Bruas Port and northwest of Melaka’s city centre. 

Positioned within the Straits of Melaka Waterfront Economic Zone — a state-initiated blueprint focused on boosting sustainable economic activity along the coastline — the site has been earmarked for projects related to industry, logistics, maritime, commerce and tourism.

The land also benefits from strong transport and logistical connectivity, with access to Melaka Sentral, the Melaka International Airport and Ayer Keroh Toll Plaza. 

LBS Bina sees the MOU as an opportunity for the group to expand its presence in Melaka and support future growth.

Meanwhile, Oriental Holdings — which is involved in various including automotive, plantations, real estate, healthcare, hospitality and plastics manufacturing — said the MOU will allow the group to leverage the expertise, resources and networks of LBS.

At the closing bell on Thursday, LBS’ shares were down half a sen or 1% at 50.5 sen, valuing the group at RM798 million. 

Meanwhile, shares of Oriental were down three sen or 0.4% at RM7.02, giving the group a market capitalisation of RM4.36 billion.

Edited ByLee Weng Khuen

data.title: Bintai Kinden says TNB has reinstated three contracts it terminated two years ago
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 15): Bintai Kinden Corp Bhd (KL:BINTAI) said Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) (KL:TENAGA) has reinstated three of 10 contracts that the utility company had previously terminated two years ago, after a series of negotiations between them.

The reinstated contracts, executed by Bintai Kinden's wholly owned Kejuruteraan Bintai Kindenko Sdn Bhd (KBK), are related to defect clearance works at PMU 275kV/33kV Grik, the 132kV Subang Jaya GIS Switching Station, and the 132kV Kuchai Lama GIS Switching Station.

"The board wishes to update that this positive development is the result of a series of negotiations between KBK and TNB, aimed at reaching an amicable resolution to the contractual matters arising from the termination of 10 contracts by TNB on June 21, 2023 and July 13, 2023 respectively," Bintai Kinden said in a bourse filing on Thursday.

Further details on the settlement and its implications will be announced in due course upon receipt of the official documentation from TNB, it said.

TNB's terminations in 2023 were reported to be linked to Bintai Kinden’s financial difficulties, which arose after the suspension or withdrawal of its banking facilities.

Bintai Kinden's subsidiary, Optimal Property Management Sdn Bhd, had defaulted on RM109 million worth of loans from MBSB Bank Bhd for a student housing project. This led to Bintai Kinden being classified under Practice Note 17 for financially distressed companies in March 2023.

In January this year, Bursa Malaysia approved Bintai Kinden’s plan to regularise its condition, which involves a private placement of 244 million new shares that represent 20% of its issued share base to raise RM19.52 million for debt repayment and working capital.

The plan also includes the granting of an option to its managing director cum chief executive officer Datuk Tay Chor Han to take up up to 146.4 million new shares in the company, and the establishment of an employees' share options scheme involving up to 15% of the company’s issued shares.

Bintai Kinden has also announced plans to reduce its share capital by up to RM160 million and to strategically shift its focus towards the construction sector to broaden its income base.

Shares of Bintai Kinden closed unchanged at eight sen on Thursday, giving the mechanical and electrical engineering services provider a market capitalisation of RM117.1 million. The counter has fallen by over 11% year-to-date.

Edited ByTan Choe Choe

data.title: Walmart warns of higher prices, withholds second-quarter profit guidance
data.description:
(May 15): Walmart will have to start raising prices later this month due to the high cost of tariffs, executives said on Thursday, even as the retailing giant's US comparable sales surpassed expectations in the first quarter.

Shares of the Bentonville, Arkansas company rose 3.5% in pre-market trading. Its stock is up more than 60% over the past year.

Walmart became the latest to avoid giving second-quarter profit guidance on Thursday due to the uncertainty around Donald Trump's tariffs that have roiled world trade.

US shoppers will start to see prices rise at the end of May and certainly in June, Walmart's chief financial officer John David Rainey said in a CNBC interview.

"We will do our best to keep our prices as low as possible but given the magnitude of the tariffs, even at the reduced levels announced this week, we aren't able to absorb all the pressure given the reality of narrow retail margins," CEO Doug McMillon said.

The retailer, however, kept its annual sales and profit forecast intact for fiscal 2026. It continues to expect adjusted earnings per share for the fiscal year ending January 2026 in the range of US$2.50 to US$2.60 and annual sales to rise between 3% and 4%.

Walmart is a bellwether of US consumer health. Its results offer clues on how the industry is navigating the economic volatility wrought by the on-and-off tariffs on several countries, including China.

Same-store sales in the first quarter grew by 4.5%, driven by increases in both transactions and unit volumes, the company said. Transactions rose 1.6%, while the average spend at the till rose 2.8%, with shoppers reaching for more dairy and pantry products, fresh food and personal care items.

Analysts, on average, were expecting a 3.94% increase in US same-store sales, according to data compiled by LSEG. Net sales rose 2.5% to US$165.6 billion (RM710.1 billion), a hair shy of estimates.

US e-commerce sales rose 21%, while globally they rose 22%. This was the first time Walmart's eCommerce business achieved a full quarter of profitability, the company said.

The retailer reported quarterly adjusted profit of 61 cents per share. Analysts, on average, were expecting 58 cents per share.

This week, the US and China reached a trade deal for 90 days that resulted in the countries slashing the tariffs imposed on each other, which was widely cheered by investors and businesses.

Many US companies in the wake of the trade war have either slashed or pulled their full-year expectations, a more cautious approach as consumers stretch their budgets even to buy everything from groceries to essentials at cheaper prices.

US consumer sentiment had ebbed for a fourth straight month in April, signalling watchful purchasing while the country's GDP contracted for the first time in three years during the first quarter fanning worries of a recession.

Walmart is known for its everyday low price strategy for regular use essentials and groceries, which has given the retailer an edge over competitors but at thin margins.

It expects second-quarter consolidated net sales to rise between 3.5% and 4.5%, compared to expectations of 3.46% growth.

As the range of near-term outcomes widens and becomes hard to predict, the company is withholding second-quarter operating income growth and earnings per share forecasts, CFO Rainey said in a statement.

"With a longer view into the full year, we believe we can navigate well and achieve our full year guidance," he added.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Singapore announces proposals to ease listing requirements
data.description:
SINGAPORE (May 15): Singapore's central bank on Thursday announced new proposals to make it easier for companies to list on its stock market, part of a rejuvenation effort to attract high-growth companies.

The Singapore Stock Exchange has struggled with low liquidity and valuations and currently has 613 listed companies, the lowest number in two decades.

The Monetary Authority of Singapore's proposals, for which it is seeking feedback, include removing a requirement for companies to provide third-party attestations along with their profit forecasts and also allowing firms to engage with retail investors and publicise their plans earlier in the process.

It also proposes reducing the amount of information a firm needs to disclose about competing entities that its directors or controlling shareholder owns.

The move comes after Singapore set up a review group in August last year to find ways of strengthening equities market development. In February, it announced measures that include a 20% tax rebate for new primary listings, a 10% rebate for secondary listings and a S$5 billion (US$3.85 billion or RM16.5 billion) programme that focuses on investing in domestic stocks.

In the second half of last year, the SGX attracted five new listings, which raised S$19.7 million in aggregate.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja

data.title: Jack Ma-backed Ant’s profit falls 31% on AI, new business costs
data.description:
(May 15): Ant Group Co’s quarterly profit fell 31% as the firm invested in artificial intelligence and other initiatives to bolster revenue growth.

The Hangzhou-based online finance firm contributed 1.76 billion yuan (US$244 million or RM1 billion) of profit to Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. Considering Alibaba’s one-third stake in Ant, that translates to an estimated 5.3 billion yuan in profit for the three months ended December 31, according to Bloomberg calculations based on Alibaba’s earnings report.

That follows a more than 50-fold surge in the previous quarter that was helped by investment gains. Ant’s earnings lag a quarter behind Alibaba’s. Ant declined to comment in an emailed statement.

Ant is seeking a turnaround by investing in initiatives including AI and building its own large language models. Sentiment for China’s tech sector is improving, following a US tariff truce this week and President Xi Jinping’s meeting with private entrepreneurs including co-founder Jack Ma in February. The Chinese billionaire has been trying to motivate staff with visits, even though Ma no longer holds any management positions at Alibaba and Ant.

In the latest breakthrough, Ant developed techniques for training AI models using Chinese-made semiconductors, which could cut costs by 20%. The company used domestic chips, including from Alibaba and Huawei Technologies Co, to train models using the Mixture of Experts machine learning approach, achieving results similar to those from Nvidia Corp chips. The company also set up a unit last year to focus on humanoid robot development.

Ant has been applying its AI tech on its own financial services. The company rolled out a “life assistant” app called Zhixiaobao, which helps people order meals, hail taxis and access other functions within its mobile payments app Alipay.

Its other new initiative Alipay Tap!, a streamlined payment process for restaurants and stores, attracted 100 million users within 11 months.

Last year, Ant enacted broad overhauls to its business, setting up independent boards for the international, database and digital technology units to pave the way for future spinoffs. The company would explore going public in Hong Kong first instead of a dual Shanghai-Hong Kong listing like it tried before it was aborted in 2020, people familiar said in 2023.

Ant has also been expanding its overseas operations to offset slowing growth at home. To fund such efforts, Ant raised US$6.5 billion in loans to refinance an offshore credit line of the same size, Bloomberg News reported in September.

Ant International operates four core businesses. Its Alipay+ connects 1.7 billion user accounts across 36 digital wallets; Antom offers payment solutions for merchants; WorldFirst enables cross-border trade payment, and Embedded Finance has an AI-powered digital lending service and helps clients with treasury and foreign exchange management.

Ant proposed buying back as much as 7.6% of its shares in 2023. Under the repurchase plan, the company’s valuation was trimmed to about US$79 billion — well off its peak of US$280 billion before regulators scrapped its planned initial public offering in 2020. The company was waiting for a financial holding company licence, which would help revive an IPO.

Read also:
Alibaba misses estimates, souring hopes of consumer revival

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja
